intro: Oryzopsis contracta, also known as contracted ricegrass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Oryzopsis contracta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and syrups for respiratory and digestive health.
